message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>The recent worldwide epidemic of Covid-19 disease, for which there is no vaccine or medications to prevent or cure it, led to the adoption of public health measures by governments and populations in most of the affected countries to avoid the contagion and its spread. These measures are known as nonpharmaceutical interventions (NPIs) and their implementation clearly produces social unrest as well as greatly affects the economy. Frequently, NPIs are implemented with an intensity quantified in an ad hoc manner. Control theory offers a worth-while tool for determining the optimal intensity of the NPIs in order to avoid the collapse of the healthcare system while keeping them as low as possible, yielding in a policymakers concrete guidance. We propose here the use of a simple proportional controller that is robust to large parametric uncertainties in the model used.</jats:p>
title: A control approach to the Covid-19 disease using a SEIHRD dynamical model

reference.unstructured: José Manuel Gutierrez and Juan Luis Varona . Análisis del Covid-19 por medio de un modelo SEIR. Technical report, Universidad de Sevilla, Sevilla, Spain, March 2020. Available at https://institucional.us.es/blogimus/2020/03/covid-19-analisis-por-medio-de-un-modelo-seir/. In Spanish.
James Holland Jones . Notes on R0 . Technical report, Stanford University, 2019. Available at http://web.stanford.edu/class/earthsys214/notes/Jones_R0_notes2019.pdf.
Jeffrey Kantor . Modeling and control of a campus COVID-19 outbreak, 2020. Available at https://github.com/jckantor/covid-19/blob/master/COVID-19.ipynb.
Mary Lowth , Coronavirus: what are asymptomatic and mild COVID-19?, March 2020. Available at https://patient.info/news-and-features/coronavirus-whatare-asymptomatic-and-mild-covid-19.
Hans Nesse. Global health – SEIR model,2020. Available at http://www.public.asu.edu/hnesse/classes/seir.html.

World Health Organization. Coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19). Technical report. Available at https://www.who.int/docs/default-source/coronaviruse/situation-reports/20200219-sitrep-30-covid-19.pdf?sfvrsn=3346b04f_2.

Greg Stewart , Klausken Van Heusden , Guy Dumont . How control theory can help us control COVID-19. Technical report, University of British Columbia, Vancouver, Canada, 2020. Available at https://spectrum.ieee.org/biomedical/diagnostics/how-control-theory-can-help-control-covid19.

Pedro V. Savi , Marcelo A. Savi , and Beatriz Borges . A mathematical description of the dynamic of Coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19): A case study of Brazil. Technical report, 2020. Available at https://arxiv.org/ftp/arxiv/papers/2004/2004.03495.pdf.
